system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

Conversation started: Friday, May 08, 2026 10:56 PM
Model: deepseek-chat
Provider: custom

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. Do NOT emit MEDIA:/path tags (those are only intercepted on messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.; on the CLI they render as literal text). When referring to a file you created or changed, just state its absolute path in plain text; the user can open it from there.

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. Auto-runs syntax checks on .py/.json/.yaml/.toml and other linted languages; only NEW errors introduced by this write are surfaced (pre-existing errors are filtered out).

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

   122|        { "icon": "shield-check", "title": "Fe Pública", "description": "Todo documento firmado ante escribano tiene valor legal pleno. No hay posibilidad de impugnación por falsedad." },
   123|        { "icon": "gavel", "title": "Seguridad Jurídica", "description": "Cada acto notarial se ajusta estrictamente a las leyes paraguayas, garantizando su validez." },
   124|        { "icon": "archive", "title": "Archivo Seguro", "description": "Los protocolos notariales se conservan por décadas. Siempre podés solicitar copias." },

   129|      "title": "¿Cómo trabajamos?",
   130|      "subtitle": "Un proceso claro y transparente para que sepas exactamente qué esperar",
   131|      "steps": [
   132|        { "number": "01", "title": "Consultá", "description": "Contactanos por WhatsApp, email o visitanos. Te asesoramos sobre la documentación necesaria para tu caso." },
   133|        { "number": "02", "title": "Preparación", "description": "Revisamos tus documentos, redactamos la minuta y preparamos todo para el acto notarial." },
   134|        { "number": "03", "title": "Firma", "description": "Agendamos la cita para la firma. Leemos el documento en voz alta y procedemos a la firma con todas las formalidades de ley." },
   135|        { "number": "04", "title": "Entrega", "description": "Recibís tu testimonio o copia debidamente protocolizada. Nos encargamos de los registros correspondientes." }

   138|    "faq": {
   139|      "title": "Preguntas Frecuentes",
   140|      "subtitle": "Respondemos tus dudas sobre servicios notariales en Paraguay",
   141|      "items": [
   142|        {
   143|          "q": "¿Qué documentos necesito para una compraventa de inmueble?",
   144|          "a": "Necesitás cédula de identidad vigente, título de propiedad (o certificado de registro fiscal), certificado de deuda municipal y de la Administración Nacional de Navegación y Puertos si corresponde, constancia de pago de impuestos inmobiliarios, y certificado de no adeudar de ANDE y ESSAP. Te guiamos en todo el proceso."
   145|        },
   146|        {
   147|          "q": "¿Cuánto cuesta un servicio notarial?",
   148|          "a": "Los costos varían según el tipo de acto y el valor del bien involucrado. Los honorarios notariales están regulados por el Colegio de Escribanos del Paraguay. Te damos un presupuesto exacto sin compromiso en tu primera consulta."
   149|        },
   150|        {
   151|          "q": "¿Qué es la Apostilla de La Haya?",
   152|          "a": "Es un método de legalización de documentos públicos para que tengan validez en países firmantes del Convenio de La Haya. Paraguay es parte desde 2014. Nosotros gestionamos la apostilla ante el Ministerio de Relaciones Exteriores para tus documentos."
   153|        },
   154|        {
   155|          "q": "¿Puedo hacer un poder desde el extranjero?",
   156|          "a": "Sí. Podés otorgar un poder desde el exterior. El documento debe ser legalizado o apostillado en el país de origen, traducido por traductor público matriculado en Paraguay si está en otro idioma, y luego presentado ante escribano para su protocolización."
   157|        },
   158|        {
   159|          "q": "¿Qué diferencia hay entre testamento ológrafo y abierto?",
   160|          "a": "El testamento ológrafo lo escribís de puño y letra y lo firmás vos mismo. El testamento abierto se otorga ante escribano y testigos, y queda protocolizado. Para el abierto no necesitás saber escribir, solo firmar. Te asesoramos sobre cuál es mejor según tu caso."
   161|        },
   162|        {
   163|          "q": "¿Cuánto tiempo toma una sucesión?",
   164|          "a": "La sucesión intestada (sin testamento) puede tomar de 3 a 6 meses aproximadamente, dependiendo de la complejidad del patrimonio y si hay acuerdo entre los herederos. La sucesión testamentaria suele ser más rápida. Aceleramos el proceso con gestión eficiente."
   165|        }
   166|      ]

   349|      "headline": "Sobre Nosotros",
   350|      "subheadline": "Compromiso con la fe pública y la seguridad jurídica en Paraguay"
   351|    },
   352|    "intro": {
   353|      "title": "Nuestra Historia",
   354|      "content": "Somos una escribanía con amplia trayectoria en el ámbito notarial paraguayo. Nuestra práctica se basa en los principios de legalidad, seguridad jurídica y servicio al cliente. Cada documento que pasa por nuestras manos es tratado con el máximo rigor profesional y cuidado. Entendemos que detrás de cada trámite hay personas con necesidades, plazos y expectativas. Por eso trabajamos con empatía, claridad y eficiencia.",
   355|      "vision": "Ser la escribanía de referencia en Paraguay, reconocida por nuestra excelencia técnica, calidez en el trato y compromiso con la seguridad jurídica.",
   356|      "mission": "Brindar servicios notariales de la más alta calidad, garantizando la legalidad y seguridad de cada acto, con un trato personalizado que hace la diferencia."
   357|    },
   358|    "values": [
   359|      { "icon": "shield-check", "title": "Integridad", "description": "Actuamos con absoluta honestidad y transparencia en cada acto notarial." },
   360|      { "icon": "gavel", "title": "Excelencia Jurídica", "description": "Nos mantenemos actualizados sobre la legislación y jurisprudencia paraguaya." },
   361|      { "icon": "users", "title": "Servicio Personalizado", "description": "Cada cliente recibe atención dedicada y explicaciones claras." },
   362|      { "icon": "clock", "title": "Eficiencia", "description": "Optimizamos cada proceso para brindarte respuestas rápidas sin sacrificar calidad." }

   396|      "title": "Contacto | {{businessName}}",

   431|  "faqPage": {
   432|    "seo": {
   433|      "title": "Preguntas Frecuentes | {{businessName}}",
   434|      "description": "Respuestas a las preguntas más comunes sobre servicios notariales en Paraguay."
   435|    },
   436|    "hero": {
   437|      "headline": "Preguntas Frecuentes",
   438|      "subheadline": "Respondemos tus dudas sobre trámites notariales"
   439|    },
   440|    "categories": [
   441|      {
   442|        "title": "Trámites Generales",
   443|        "items": [
   444|          {
   445|            "q": "¿Qué documentos necesito para identificarme ante el escribano?",
   446|            "a": "Necesitás tu cédula de identidad civil vigente (paraguaya) o pasaporte vigente (extranjeros). Para actos que requieran capacidad especial, pueden solicitarse documentos adicionales."
   447|          },
   448|          {
   449|            "q": "¿Cuánto cuesta una consulta inicial?",
   450|            "a": "La consulta inicial es sin costo. Te asesoramos sobre la documentación, el proceso y los costos del servicio notarial que necesitás."
   451|          },
   452|          {
   453|            "q": "¿Puedo firmar un documento si estoy en el extranjero?",
   454|            "a": "Sí. Podés firmar ante un notario en tu país de residencia, apostillar/legalizar el documento y enviarlo a Paraguay. También podemos coordinar videoconferencia para ciertos actos."
   455|          }
   456|        ]
   457|      },
   458|      {
   459|        "title": "Inmuebles y Propiedades",
   460|        "items": [
   461|          {
   462|            "q": "¿Qué impuestos debo pagar al comprar un inmueble?",
   463|            "a": "El comprador paga el Impuesto a la Transferencia de Inmuebles (ITI) del 4% sobre el valor fiscal actualizado. También hay gastos de registro y honorarios notariales. Te damos el presupuesto completo antes de iniciar."
   464|          },
   465|          {
   466|            "q": "¿Es obligatorio firmar escritura pública para comprar un inmueble?",
   467|            "a": "Sí, según el Código Civil paraguayo, la transferencia de dominio de inmuebles debe hacerse por escritura pública. Es la única forma de inscribir la propiedad a tu nombre en la Dirección General de Registros Públicos."
   468|          }
   469|        ]
   470|      },
   471|      {
   472|        "title": "Empresas y Sociedades",
   473|        "items": [
   474|          {
   475|            "q": "¿Qué tipo de sociedad me conviene?",
   476|            "a": "Depende de tu actividad, capital y cantidad de socios. La SRL es ideal para PYMES, la SA para empresas grandes, y la EAS para pequeños emprendedores. Te asesoramos sin costo."
   477|          },
   478|          {
   479|            "q": "¿Cuánto tiempo toma constituir una sociedad?",
   480|            "a": "Aproximadamente 2 a 4 semanas, incluyendo la escritura pública, inscripción en la DGRRCC y obtención del RUC. Podemos hacer el proceso en paralelo para agilizar."
   481|          }
   482|        ]
   483|      }
   484|    ]
   485|  },
   486|  "privacidadPage": {
   487|    "seo": {
   488|      "title": "Política de Privacidad | {{businessName}}",
   489|      "description": "Política de privacidad y tratamiento de datos personales de {{businessName}}."
   490|    },
   491|    "hero": {
   492|      "headline": "Política de Privacidad",
   493|      "subheadline": "Cómo protegemos tus datos personales"
   494|    },
   495|    "content": {
   496|      "sections": [
   497|        {
   498|          "title": "1. Información que recopilamos",
   499|          "text": "Recopilamos información personal que nos proporcionás voluntariamente al contactarnos, como nombre, correo electrónico, número de teléfono y datos de identificación necesarios para los servicios notariales."
   500|        },
   501|        {
   502|          "title": "2. Uso de la información",
   503|          "text": "Usamos tu información exclusivamente para: brindar los servicios notariales solicitados, comunicarnos con vos sobre tu trámite, cumplir con obligaciones legales y regulatorias, y mejorar nuestros servicios."
   504|        },
   505|        {
   506|          "title": "3. Protección de datos",
   507|          "text": "Implementamos medidas de seguridad técnicas y organizativas para proteger tus datos personales contra acceso no autorizado, pérdida o destrucción."
   508|        },
   509|        {
   510|          "title": "4. Confidencialidad notarial",
   511|          "text": "Como escribanos, estamos sujetos al secreto profesional y a la confidencialidad notarial. La información que compartís con nosotros está protegida por ley."
   512|        },
   513|        {
   514|          "title": "5. Tus derechos",
   515|          "text": "Tenés derecho a acceder, rectificar, cancelar y oponerte al tratamiento de tus datos personales. Contactanos para ejercer estos derechos."
   516|        }
   517|      ]
   518|    }

All 13 files created. Here's a summary:

## Summary

**What I did:** Created all 13 missing component files in `/root/escribania-paraguay/components/` following the existing patterns from the codebase.

**Files created (13 total):**

1. **`intro-section.tsx`** — Server component. Centered text block on bg-surface with section-padding-sm.

2. **`service-categories.tsx`** — Server component. 2-column grid of service category cards from the /servicios page data. Each card shows icon, title, description, features list, and links to `/servicios/{id}`. Uses the same icon mapping as services-preview for: scroll-text, file-signature, file-text, stamp, landmark, building.

3. **`about-intro.tsx`** — Server component. Title + body text at top, then two side-by-side cards (Vision with Eye icon, Mission with Target icon).

4. **`values-section.tsx`** — Server component. 4-column grid of values cards on bg-background. Icon mapping: shield-check→ShieldCheck, gavel→Gavel, users→Users, clock→Clock.

5. **`team-section.tsx`** — Server component. 3-column grid of team member cards with placeholder circle (User icon), name, role, description, education.

6. **`contact-info.tsx`** — Server component. 4-column grid of contact info cards with MapPin, Phone, Mail, Clock icons.

7. **`contact-form-section.tsx`** — Client component (`'use client'`). Two-column layout (text left, form right). Renders form fields by type (text, email, tel, select, textarea). On submit shows success message "Gracias por tu consulta. Te responderemos a la brevedad." Submit button with accent color.

8. **`faq-categories.tsx`** — Client component (`'use client'`). Multi-category FAQ with accordion per category using useState for expand/collapse. Same accordion pattern as faq-section.tsx but organized by category title headers.

9. **`privacy-content.tsx`** — Server component. Simple list of sections with title and text on bg-surface.

10. **`service-detail-hero.tsx`** — Server component. Dark hero variant (bg-primary, white text) with heading + subheading, no CTA buttons. Matches the hero dark variant style.

11. **`service-benefits.tsx`** — Server component. 4-column benefits grid on bg-surface, same structure as trust-section but with different background.

12. **`service-items-list.tsx`** — Server component. List of service features with Check icons, 2-column grid on desktop, 1 on mobile, on bg-surface-alt.

13. **`service-process.tsx`** — Server component. Process steps with number circles and descriptions (no step titles), same visual pattern as process-section.tsx. Connecting line on desktop.

**Patterns followed:**
- All use `@/components/animations/scroll-reveal` for reveal animations
- All use `container-page`, `section-padding`, `section-padding-sm` CSS classes
- All use `font-heading`, `font-accent`, `text-accent`, `text-muted`, `text-foreground` utility classes
- Icon mapping pattern consistent with trust-section.tsx and services-preview.tsx
- CSS variable references match globals.css
- Proper TypeScript interfaces defined inline
- Lucide-react for all icons
- bg-[var(--color-*)] pattern where Tailwind v4 class syntax is needed (service-detail-hero uses `bg-[var(--color-primary)]`)
